skill.name: caveman-help
skill.description: Quick-reference card for all caveman modes, skills, and commands. One-shot display, not a persistent mode. Trigger: /caveman-help, "caveman help", "what caveman commands", "how do I use caveman".
skill.summary: Reference card showing all caveman mode triggers, skills, and configuration options in one view.
skill.author: juliusbrussee

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 26 issues with a risk score of 100/100, but all are false positives. The skill is a pure Markdown reference card with no executable code. Backtick characters are Markdown inline code formatting (e.g., /caveman lite), not shell execution. The single URL links to the project's own GitHub documentation. File path references (~/.config/caveman/config.json) document user configuration locations, not skill behavior. Crypto-related detections on YAML frontmatter and section headings are scanner errors. No security risks found after manual review.

content.user_title: View caveman mode commands and skills
content.value_statement: AI coding agents produce verbose responses that increase token costs. This quick-reference card lists all caveman mode triggers, skills, and configuration in one view so you can compress agent output by 65-75 percent.
actual_capabilities: Display a reference card listing all available caveman intensity modes (lite, full, ultra, wenyan variants)
Show trigger commands for each mode and sub-skill (commit, review, compress, help)
Explain how to deactivate caveman mode with natural language phrases
Document configuration options for setting a default mode via environment variable or config file
Link to full project documentation on GitHub
limitations: This is a one-shot display skill and does not activate or persist any mode
Does not write flag files or modify session state
Requires the main caveman skill to be installed for mode commands to work
Reference only — actual compression behavior is handled by other caveman sub-skills

use_cases.title: New user onboarding
use_cases.description: A developer installs caveman for the first time and needs to see all available modes and commands in one place without reading full documentation.

use_cases.title: Command lookup during work
use_cases.description: An experienced user forgets the exact trigger syntax for wenyan mode or the compress sub-skill and needs a quick reference without leaving their coding session.

use_cases.title: Mode configuration setup
use_cases.description: A user wants to change their default caveman mode from full to lite and needs to know the environment variable and config file options available.

prompt_templates.title: Show all caveman commands
prompt_templates.scenario: You just installed caveman and want to see all available modes and commands.

prompt_templates.title: Quick reference card
prompt_templates.scenario: You are in a coding session and need a quick reminder of mode triggers.

prompt_templates.title: Check configuration options
prompt_templates.prompt: What caveman commands are available and how do I set my default mode?
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to change the default caveman mode and need configuration instructions.

prompt_templates.title: Find deactivation method
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I turn off caveman mode?
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to return to normal AI responses and need the deactivation commands.

output: Modes table: Lite, Full, Ultra, Wenyan-Lite, Wenyan-Full, Wenyan-Ultra with trigger commands and behavior descriptions
Skills table: caveman-commit, caveman-review, caveman-compress, caveman-help with triggers
Deactivation instructions: say 'stop caveman' or 'normal mode'
Configuration: environment variable and config file options for setting default mode
Reference card displayed with all six intensity modes listed
Sub-skills documented with exact trigger syntax
Config instructions showing CAVEMAN_DEFAULT_MODE env var and ~/.config/caveman/config.json path

best_practices: Use /caveman-help as a quick reference rather than memorizing all mode trigger commands
Set your preferred default mode via the CAVEMAN_DEFAULT_MODE environment variable for consistent behavior across sessions
Use natural language phrases like 'stop caveman' or 'activate caveman' as alternatives to slash commands
anti_patterns: Do not expect this skill to activate or persist any caveman mode — it is a one-shot display only
Do not confuse this help card with the main caveman skill that controls AI response formatting
Do not modify or delete the config file at ~/.config/caveman/config.json — this skill only documents its location

faq.question: What is the difference between caveman-help and the main caveman skill?
faq.answer: caveman-help displays a reference card with all commands and modes. The main caveman skill actually changes how the AI responds, compressing output into short, direct language.

faq.question: How do I activate caveman mode using this skill?
faq.answer: This skill does not activate modes. It only shows you the commands. Use /caveman to activate full mode, or /caveman lite or /caveman ultra for other intensity levels.

faq.question: Can I set a default mode that loads automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. Set the CAVEMAN_DEFAULT_MODE environment variable to your preferred mode, or create a config file at ~/.config/caveman/config.json with your defaultMode setting.

faq.question: How do I turn off caveman mode permanently?
faq.answer: Set the default mode to 'off' in your config file at ~/.config/caveman/config.json. This disables auto-activation on session start. You can still activate manually with /caveman when needed.

faq.question: What are the wenyan modes?
faq.answer: Wenyan modes use Classical Chinese writing style (文言文) instead of English. Wenyan-lite applies light compression in classical style. Wenyan-full uses full classical terseness. Wenyan-ultra applies extreme compression in ancient scholar style.

faq.question: Does this skill persist anything to my filesystem?
faq.answer: No. This is a one-shot display skill. It reads no files, writes no flag files, and changes no configuration. It only shows you the reference card in caveman style.
